title: Chopper Begins to Believe
description: On pages 18–19, this part focuses on chopper begins to believe, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Chopper Begins to Believe. Chopper learns what Hiriluk believes about medicine, hope, and the cherry blossom dream that can heal a country. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Why must we go to such unethical lengths for these "doctor-Hunts"...!?

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: Silence fool! I never asked for your opinion, Dalton!

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: Besides, Hiriluk is nothing but a criminal! My hippo would know more about the hippo-cratic oath than that quack!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: As such, it's only natural that he should be executed at once!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Now if you understand, then you'll carry out the plan at once! That's an order!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: How could someone so kind be unable to become a doctor...!?

summary: This part highlights chopper begins to believe and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
